title: The Region That Wants Out
subtitle: Hold the union, or let it breathe.

turns.prompt: A wealthy region demands full independence. Half the country cheers; the other half is appalled.

choices.label: Grant a legal independence referendum
choices.reaction: Democracy honoured. The map suddenly feels provisional.

choices.label: Ban the vote and assert central control

delayed.headline: The suppression only hardens the independence movement

choices.label: Offer sweeping devolved powers instead
choices.flavour: Stay, but on your own terms.
choices.reaction: A middle path. Hardliners on both sides grumble.

turns.prompt: The standoff cools into negotiation. There's space to redesign the deal.

choices.label: Redirect wealth to poorer regions

delayed.headline: The wealthy region bristles at subsidising the rest

choices.reaction: Solidarity funded. The donor regions seethe.

choices.label: Let regions keep more of their own taxes
choices.reaction: Local control grows. The gaps between regions widen.

choices.label: Launch a national unity campaign
choices.reaction: Flags and festivals. Skeptics call it spin.

turns.prompt: A contested vote splits the country down the middle. Both sides claim the mandate.

choices.label: Negotiate an orderly separation
choices.flavour: Part as friends, if you can.
choices.reaction: A velvet divorce begins. The borders harden.

choices.label: Refound the nation as a loose federation
choices.reaction: A new constitution drafted. The details will be war.

choices.label: Use every lever to hold the union together
choices.flavour: Whatever it takes to stay one country.
choices.reaction: The union endures, for now, under enormous strain.

turns.prompt: The moves you made to manage the crisis have set the terms of the rupture.

memory_callbacks.text: The crackdown you ordered turned a fringe cause into a national rupture.
The transfers you mandated have the rich regions threatening to leave for good.
Holding the union by force has drained your legitimacy dry.
The separation you began is proving messier and costlier than you promised.

choices.label: Build a fair, durable federal settlement
choices.reaction: Power shared by design. It will be tested constantly.

choices.label: Recentralise power to end the chaos
choices.reaction: One country, firmly held. Quietly resentful.

choices.label: Let the region go and stabilise the rest
choices.reaction: A smaller country, more at peace with itself.

turns.prompt: The constitutional storm passes. What shape is your country left in?

choices.label: A confident, decentralised federation
choices.reaction: Many peoples under one loose roof. It mostly holds.

choices.label: A strong, unified central state
choices.reaction: Orderly and whole. Some still dream of leaving.

choices.label: Smaller, richer, and finally at peace
choices.reaction: A clean break that paid off — for those who stayed.
